message:
fix(serve,robot): session name validation, race-safe config endpoints, policy check error logging, and alert tracker cleanup

Server input validation (pipelines.go, server.go):
- Add tmux.ValidateSessionName() calls to handleRunPipeline,
  handleExecPipeline, handleResumePipeline, and handleCreateSessionV1
  to reject malformed session names at the API boundary before they
  reach tmux — returns 400 with a descriptive error message

Race condition in config endpoints (server.go):
- handleGetConfigV1: snapshot corsAllowedOrigins and projectDir under
  s.mu.Lock() before building the response, instead of reading them
  outside the lock where concurrent PATCH requests could see
  inconsistent values
- handlePatchConfigV1: snapshot the updated values under the same lock
  used to apply them, then use the snapshots for logging and the
  response body — ensures the logged values match what was actually
  written

Policy check error handling (server.go):
- Refactor handlePaneInputV1, handleAgentSendV1: when
  policy.LoadOrDefault() fails, log a warning with the error instead
  of silently allowing the request through
- Add policy check to handleAgentInterruptV1 for the follow-up
  message field, which was previously unchecked
- All three handlers now use the if-else-if pattern instead of nested
  if blocks for clarity

Alert tracker (sections.go):
- Remove redundant nil check on GetGlobalTracker() return value since
  the tracker is always initialized — simplifies the control flow and
  removes a dead code path that returned an "unavailable" omission

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
